title: Deutsch A1 — Absolute Beginner
description: Start your German journey from zero. This course covers the alphabet, basic greetings, numbers, colors, everyday vocabulary, and simple sentence construction. Ideal for those who have never studied German before.
shortDescription: Master German fundamentals: greetings, numbers, basic grammar, and everyday phrases — no prior knowledge needed.
instructorBio: Anna is a certified German language teacher (DaF) with 10 years of experience teaching students from beginners to advanced levels. She holds a Master's degree in German Linguistics from Humboldt University Berlin.
whatYouLearn: Introduce yourself and others in German
Count, tell the time, and use dates
Describe your family, home, and daily routine
Order food and drinks at a restaurant
Use basic German grammar (nominative & accusative case)
requirements: Stable internet connection for online sessions
Notebook and pen for in-class exercises

title: Deutsch A2 — Elementary
description: Build on your A1 foundation. This course expands your vocabulary, introduces past tense (Perfekt), dative case, modal verbs, and conversational skills for everyday situations like shopping, travel, and work.
shortDescription: Expand beyond basics: past tense, dative case, modal verbs, and real-life conversations.
instructorBio: Anna is a certified German language teacher (DaF) with 10 years of experience teaching students from beginners to advanced levels.
whatYouLearn: Talk about past events using Perfekt tense
Use modal verbs (können, müssen, wollen, dürfen)
Navigate shopping, travel, and appointments in German
Understand the dative case and prepositions
Hold basic conversations on familiar topics
requirements: Completed A1 course or equivalent knowledge
Stable internet connection for online sessions

title: Deutsch B1 — Intermediate
description: Reach conversational independence. The B1 course covers the Konjunktiv II, genitive case, complex sentence structures, and prepares you for the Goethe-Institut B1 certificate exam. Topics include work, media, society, and travel.
shortDescription: Achieve conversational independence and prepare for the Goethe B1 certificate.
instructorBio: Thomas is a professional German trainer with 15 years of experience. He specialises in exam preparation and business German, and has helped hundreds of students achieve their Goethe-Institut certificates.
whatYouLearn: Express wishes, hypotheticals and politeness with Konjunktiv II
Understand and produce complex texts on various topics
Use relative clauses and subordinate conjunctions
Discuss current events, work, and social issues
Prepare for and pass the Goethe B1 certificate
Write formal letters and structured arguments
requirements: Solid A2 knowledge or completion of A2 course
Motivation to study independently between sessions
materials: Course workbook and exam practice book (included)
